evals.prompt: Give me a weekly AI brief in slide format.
evals.expected_output: A 5-8 slide .pptx briefing curated from list_latest, each model slide enriched via get_model, with a download URL.
assertions: list_latest is called once with limit ≥ 10
Curation drops trivial variants (e.g. duplicate previews)
get_model is called in parallel for each curated pick
The deck has between 5 and 8 slides (cover + content + closing)
container_file_download_url is called and returned with TTL note
Local-model picks render as 'free (local)' rather than '$null/MTok'

evals.prompt: Monthly AI brief in markdown, and save it to my library.
evals.expected_output: Markdown output with curated picks; the brief is written to a file in the container, a download URL is generated, and upload_url stores it in totext-cloud.
assertions: list_latest is called with a higher limit (≥20) for a monthly window
container_file_download_url is generated for the markdown file
upload_url is called against totext-cloud with the brief's URL
The user is told the upload is async (entities/edges take time)

evals.expected_output: list_latest is called with provider='anthropic'; output only includes Anthropic models.
assertions: Every pick in the brief has provider_id='anthropic'
If only 1-2 models are returned, the brief acknowledges that explicitly rather than padding
